name: Pot Stickers Filling
recipeIngredient: 1/2 small Head Chinese (napa) cabbage, cored and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine pork, cabbage, green onion, ginger, water chestnuts, salt, sugar, white pepper, and sesame oil in a bowl. Refrigerate until ready to use.
2. To assemble pot stickers, spoon 1 level tablespoon of filling into the center of each pancake. Fold the dough over to form a half-circle and pleat the edges firmly together. Pinch shut to seal, then decorate with the point of a dinner knife.
3. Heat cast-iron or wok (or other heavy-bottom skillet) over moderate heat. Add 3 tablespoons vegetable oil and swirl to coat the bottom.
4. When the oil is hot, place the pot stickers, seam side up, in the skillet. Shake for 30 seconds (you may need to do this in two batches).
5. Pour in 1 cup water and cover with a lid. Gently boil over moderate heat for 7 to 8 minutes. If more oil is needed, add it carefully.
6. Remove the skillet from the heat and use a spatula to lift out the pot stickers onto serving platters.
7. Turn the pot stickers over (dark side up) and serve with dipping sauce.
TIPS: The pork filling may appear pink even when fully cooked due to myoglobin in the meat. Cabbage water can be squeezed out during preparation using a colander or cheesecloth before freezing. Roll the pancakes so that the center is slightly thicker than the edges for better browning. Alternatively, steam the pot stickers over boiling water for about 12 minutes instead of pan-frying.
Per Pot Sticker: 57 calories (20% from protein, 60 percent from carbohydrate, 20 percent from fat), 3 grams protein, 6 milligrams cholesterol, 95 grams sodium. Exchanges 1/2 bread.
